title: Havanoor Shekappa S/O Mallerappa vs Havanoor Shivappa S/O Late Shankrappa on 30 September, 2013
body: Havanoor Shekappa S/O Mallerappa vs Havanoor Shivappa S/O Late Shankrappa on 30 September, 2013 Author: S.N.Satyanarayana Bench: S.N.Satyanarayana 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA DHARWAD BENCH DATED THIS THE 30TH DAY OF SEPTEMBER, 2013 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR.JUSTICE S.N.SATYANARAYANA RSA NO.1621/2008 (PARTN.) BETWEEN 1. HAVANOOR SHEKAPPA, S/O MALLERAPPA, AGED ABOUT 56 YEARS, OCC AGRICULTURIST, R/O KATTEBENNUR VILLAE HADAGALI, BELLARY DISTRICT. 587 305. 2. HAVANOOR NEELAPPA, S/O MALLERAPPA, AGED ABOUT 51 YEARS OCC AGRICULTURIST, R/O KATTEBENNUR VILLAE HADAGALI, BELLARY DISTRICT. 587 305. 3. HAVANOOR BASAPPA,S/O MALLERAPPA AGED ABOUT 31 YEARS, OCC AGRICULTURIST, R/O KATTEBENNUR VILLAE HADAGALI, BELLARY DISTRICT. 587 305. ... APPELLANTS (By Sri ARAVIND H, ADV. FOR APPELLANTS) 2 AND 1. HAVANOOR SHIVAPPA, S/O LATE SHANKRAPPA, AGED ABOUT 51 YEARS, OCC AGRICULTURIST, R/O KATTEBENNUR VILLAGE, HADAGALI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT. 587 305. 2. SMT.HAVANOOR ERAVVA, W/O LATE MALLEREAPPA, AGED ABOUT 71 YEARS KATTEBENNUR VILLAGE, HADAGALI,BELLARY DISTRICT. 587 305. 3. HAVANOOR MELAGIRIYAPPA,S/O MALLERAPPA, AGED ABOUT 53 YEARS OCC AGRICULTURIST. R/O KATTEBENNUR VILLAE HADAGALI, BELLARY DISTRICT. 587 305. 4. HAVANOOR GANGAMMA, W/O NINGAPPA D/O LATE MALLERAPPA, AGED ABOUT 41 YEARS, OCC AGRICULTURIST. R/O DUGGAWATHI VILLAE HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, DAVANGERE DISTRICT. 577 605. 5. SMT BADAPPANAVARA MAHADEVAKKA, W/O NINGAPPA,D/O MALLERAPPA, AGED ABOUT 41 YEARS, HOLE ITTIGE VILLAGE, SHIRAHATTI VILLAGE, GADAG DIST. 582 101. ... RESPONDENTS 3 (By Sri SADIQ N GOODWALA, ADV. FOR R1, R.2 DEAD. APPELLANT Nos.1 TO 3 & R.3 TO R.5 ARE LRS OF DECEASED R.2, R.3, R.4 AND R.5 SERVED AND UNREPRESENTED) THIS RSA IS FILED UNDER SECTION 100 R/W ORDER 42, RULE 1 OF CPC , 1908 AGAINST THE JUDGEMENT & DECREE DATED: 19/03/2008 PASSED IN R.A.NO.75/2007 ON THE FILE OF THE ADDL. CIVIL JUDGE (SR.DN) AT: HOSPET, DISMISSING THE APPEAL AND CONFIRMING THE JUDGEMENT & DECREE DATED: 05/07/2007 IN O.S NO. 23/2005 ON THE FILE OF THE LEARNED CIVIL JUDGE (JR.DN) AND J.M.F.C. AT HADAGALI, DECREEING THE SUIT FILED FOR PARTITION AND SEPARATE POSSESSION. THIS RSA COMING ON FOR ADMISSION, THIS DAY, THE COURT DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING: JUDGMENT The defendant Nos.2, 4, 7 in O.S.No.23/2005 on the file of the Civil Judge (Jr.Dn), Hadagali, have come up in this second appeal impugning the concurrent findings of both the courts below in decreeing the suit of the plaintiff for the relief of partition and separate possession of suit B-schedule property bearing No.255/G measuring 5 acres 58 cents, situated in Kattebennur village of Hadagali Taluk in Bellary District. 4 2. Admittedly, the plaintiff and defendants are members of the same family, i.e. plaintiff is second son of Shankarappa and defendants are wife and children of his elder brother Mallareppa. The case of the plaintiff is that, in the year 1962 there was a partition in the family of plaintiff and his brothers and their father Shankarappa, wherein certain lands were allotted to the share of plaintiff as well as his elder brother Mallareppa. At the time of said partition, the suit schedule property, i.e. property bearing No.255/G measuring 5 acres 58 cents was retained for maintenance of their father Shankarappa, and after death of Shankarappa the plaintiff was entitled to seek partition of 50% of his share in the said property. 3. In the said proceedings, defendants entered appearance and contended that the suit schedule property was the subject matter of an earlier suit in O. S.112 /1990, in which the plaintiff who had sought for declaration and injunction has lost the said suit and suffered a decree, and therefore he is not entitled to seek any relief in the present 5 suit. However, it is seen that O. S.112 / 90 was in respect of declaration and the present suit O. S.23 /2005 is for partition and separate possession of half share in the suit property. Though in the original suit a defence is taken to the effect that suit schedule property is bequeathed in favour of 7th defendant by deceased Shankarappa-father of plaintiff and his elder brother Mallareppa, defendants have failed to establish the execution of Will and bequest made in favour of 7th defendant before trial Court. In that view of the matter, the Trial Court decreed the suit in granting 50% share in the suit schedule property bearing No.255/G of Kattebennur village to plaintiff. 4. Being aggrieved by the judgment and decree passed by the Trial Court, defendants 1, 2, 4 and 7 challenged the judgment and decree dated 5.7.2007 passed in O. S.23 /2005 in R.A.75/2007 on the file of Addl.Civil Judge (Sr.Dn), Hospet. In the lower appellate Court also the judgment and decree passed by the Trial Court is confirmed accepting the finding given on issue Nos.1 to 8 framed 6 therein, by answering points for consideration 1 to 3 in the negative and consequently dismissing the appeal filed by defendants-1, 2, 4 and 7, by its judgment and decree dated 19.3.2008. 5. As against the concurrent findings of both the courts below, this Second Appeal is filed by defendants 2, 4 and 7. Heard the learned Counsel for appellants. Perused the judgments of both the courts below. On going through the same, it is seen that the fact that suit schedule property, i.e. land bearing R.S.No.255/G was the property of joint family is not in dispute. It is also not in dispute that the suit schedule property was given to the share of plaintiff's father Shankarappa and the said Shankarappa died subsequently and in respect of the property which was in possession and enjoyment of Shankarappa, the present suit is filed. 6. In the proceedings before the Trial Court, an attempt was made by defendants to rely upon the judgment and decree passed in O. S.112 /1990, i.e. another suit for 7 declaration and injunction and also the defence that the suit schedule property is bequeathed by deceased Shankarappa in favour of defendant-7, i.e. son of Mallareppa, is not found favour in both the courts below, in as much, the Will is not property established and the right of plaintiff to seek declaration of 50% share in respect of the suit schedule property is not properly assailed. On going through the judgment of both the courts below, it is clearly seen that the reason given therein in accepting the right of plaintiff with reference to his half share in the suit properties cannot be disturbed in this Second Appeal, for the reason no grounds are made out by defendants-2, 4 and 7 as against concurrent findings of facts by both the courts below. Also no substantial question of law arise for consideration against finding of facts by both the courts below. Accordingly, this Second Appeal filed by defendants-2, 4 and 7 does not merit admission and same is dismissed. SD/- JUDGE .sub/
